manage_persons.instructions: Select two persons to merge. The first selected (orange) will be merged into the second (green).

Merge "{source}" into "{target}"?

This will move all faces from "{source}" to "{target}" and delete "{source}".
manage_persons.confirm_delete:
Delete "{name}"?

This will remove the person and unassign all {count} photos from this person. The faces will remain in the database but will need to be re-clustered.

compare.title: Compare Photos

strategy_help.title: Comparison Strategies
strategy_help.uncertainty_desc: Selects pairs with nearly identical aggregate scores. Best for calibrating weights - your preference reveals which metrics matter when the algorithm can't tell the difference.
strategy_help.boundary_desc: Focuses on photos scoring 5.5-8.5, the "gray zone" between good and mediocre. Helps refine the threshold between acceptable and rejects.
strategy_help.active_desc: Prioritizes photos that have been compared fewer times. Ensures all photos contribute to the learned scores and improves overall coverage.
strategy_help.random_desc: Selects completely random pairs. Good for getting unbiased coverage and avoiding strategy-specific biases in your comparisons.

compare.no_photos: No photos available for comparison in this category.

tooltips.equal_quality: Both photos are equally good (or equally bad)
tooltips.cant_compare: Can't decide or photos are too different to compare
tooltips.suggest_weights: Suggest optimal weights based on your comparisons
tooltips.suggest_weights_disabled: Need at least {min} comparisons ({count} so far)
tooltips.apply_config: Save suggested weights to config file
tooltips.recalculate: Recalculate all categories and aggregate scores using current config
tooltips.reset_weights: Reset weight sliders to original values
tooltips.reset_all: Clear all comparison data and start fresh

weights.title: Weight Preview
weights.already_good: Current weights already achieve good accuracy - changes not recommended.

help.title: How Weight Learning Works
help.step1_desc: Each time you pick a winner, the system learns your preferences using the Bradley-Terry model, which calculates a "true quality score" for each photo based on win/loss patterns.
help.step2_title: 2. Suggest Weights optimizes the weight combination
help.step2_desc: The algorithm finds the best ratio of weights that makes the combined score match your preferences. It uses mathematical optimization (SLSQP) to maximize how well the weighted sum predicts your choices.
help.step3_positive: Positive correlation (+0.5 to +1.0) = weights rank photos similar to your preferences
help.step3_zero: Near zero (-0.2 to +0.2) = not enough data or mixed signals
help.step3_negative: Negative correlation = weights rank opposite to your preferences
help.step4_title: 4. Apply to save, Recalculate to update
help.step4_desc: Click "Apply to Config" to save weights, then "Recalculate Scores" to update all photo scores in the database.
help.tip: Do 30+ comparisons per category for suggestions (50+ recommended for accuracy). Mix strategies for best coverage. Ties are now included in optimization.

modal.title: Change Category
modal.no_conflicts: Photo already matches target category filters

confirm.reset_body:
This will permanently delete:
- {count} pairwise comparisons
- All learned weight suggestions
- All session statistics

This cannot be undone.
confirm.recalculate_body:
This will:
- Re-determine categories for all photos based on config filters
- Recalculate aggregate scores using current category weights
- Update the database with new scores

Category weights from: {category}

This may take several minutes for large databases.
confirm.apply_body:
Category: {category}

Weight changes:
{changes}

This will:
- Update scoring_config.json with new weights
- Create a backup of the current config
- NOT recalculate existing scores (use "Recalculate Scores" after)

Save these weights?

notify.weights_saved: Weights saved for "{category}"! Click "Recalculate" to apply to all photos.

gear_profile_help.description: Shows the most commonly used camera, lens, and average shooting settings (ISO, aperture, focal length) for each photo category. This helps identify which gear and settings you gravitate toward for different types of photography.

weight_impact.title: Weight Definition vs Actual Impact
weight_impact.description: Blue bars show your configured weights. Green bars show Pearson correlation with the aggregate score — a dimension at 0% weight can still show impact if it naturally correlates with other weighted dimensions. Large gaps suggest misaligned weights.
weight_impact.empty: Click Load to compare weight definitions with their actual scoring impact.

breakdown.title: Category Breakdown
breakdown.select_category: Select a category to view details

weights.title: Category Weights
weights.radar_help: Each axis represents one of the 12 scoring dimensions. The polygon's shape shows how much each dimension contributes to the aggregate score for that category. A wider polygon means more dimensions are weighted heavily. Comparing polygons reveals which categories emphasize similar dimensions (overlapping shapes) or have very different scoring strategies (different shapes). Select up to 5 categories to compare.
weights.heatmap_help: A matrix of all categories (rows) and scoring dimensions (columns). Cell brightness indicates the weight percentage — brighter green means a higher contribution to the aggregate. This gives a bird's-eye view to spot patterns: which dimensions are universally important, which are category-specific, and where weights might need rebalancing.
weights.editor_intro: Edit how the aggregate score is computed for a category. Each dimension's score (0-10) is multiplied by its weight percentage, then summed to produce the final aggregate. Modifiers apply additional bonuses or penalty adjustments.
weights.weights_hint: How much each dimension contributes to the aggregate score. Should total 100% for a balanced weighted average.
weights.save_note: Updates config file. Recompute to apply to all photos in this category.
weights.corr_hint: Compares your slider weights (blue) against actual Pearson correlation with aggregate score (green/red). Mismatches reveal dimensions with high weight but low real impact, or vice versa.
weights.preview_hint: Estimated aggregate score distribution with the current slider values compared to actual scores.
weights.modifiers_hint: Fine-tune scoring behavior beyond the weighted average. Multipliers scale penalties (0.5 = half penalty, 2 = double). Bonuses are added to the final score.

correlations.title: Score-Weight Correlations
correlations.heatmap_help: Pearson r measures how much a dimension's raw score actually moves in sync with the aggregate score (-1 to +1). Green = positive correlation (higher dimension score leads to higher aggregate), red = negative. A bright cell means strong influence. Compare this to your configured weights: if a dimension has high weight but low correlation, it may not be differentiating photos effectively in that category.
correlations.detail_help: Side-by-side comparison for one category. The green bars show the weight you configured (as %), the blue bars show the actual Pearson correlation. Look for mismatches: a tall green bar with a short blue bar means you're weighting a dimension heavily but it has little real impact on score variation. A tall blue bar with a short green bar means an influential dimension is under-weighted.
correlations.select_category: Click a category to view details

help.breakdown: Shows how your photos are distributed across categories. The bar chart displays photo counts per category sorted by volume. Select a category from the dropdown to see its average scores across dimensions and the aggregate score distribution histogram.
help.weights: Each category has 12 weight dimensions that control how the aggregate score is calculated. The radar chart lets you visually compare weight profiles across up to 5 categories. The heatmap gives a full overview of all categories at a glance. In edition mode, you can adjust weights with sliders (should sum to 100%) and modify scoring modifiers like bonus points or penalty toggles.
help.correlations: Shows how strongly each weight dimension actually correlates with the aggregate score using Pearson r (-1 to +1). Green cells indicate positive correlation, red cells negative. Click a category row to see a side-by-side comparison of configured weight % versus actual correlation - this highlights misalignments where a high weight has low impact or vice versa.
help.overlap: Categories are evaluated in priority order - a photo is assigned to the first matching category. This tab shows which category filters overlap: the top table lists pairs of categories whose filters both match the same photos. The summary table shows how many photos match each filter versus how many are actually assigned (the difference being captured by higher-priority categories).

mod_hints.bonus: Fixed points added to the final score (e.g. 0.3 = +0.3 pts)
mod_hints.noise_tolerance: Scales the noise penalty (0.5 = half penalty, good for night/astro)
mod_hints.iso_tolerance: Scales the high-ISO penalty (lower = more forgiving)
mod_hints.saturation_bonus: Reward for high color saturation (concerts, sunsets)
mod_hints.apply_blink_penalty: Penalize photos where subjects have closed eyes

edition.unlock_description: Enter the edition password to enable editing features.

comparison.title: Weight Tuning
comparison.no_preview: Click Refresh Preview to see results
comparison.correlation_description: Pearson r between each dimension and aggregate score
comparison.compare_description: Pick the better photo to help calibrate scoring weights
comparison.no_more_pairs: No more pairs available for comparison
comparison.snapshots_description: Snapshots are saved server-side and scoped to the current category. Create a snapshot before experimenting with weights, then restore it to go back to a known-good state. Snapshots persist across sessions and devices.
comparison.save_tooltip: Save current weight configuration to scoring_config.json
comparison.optimize_tooltip: Suggest optimal weights based on your pairwise comparisons
comparison.reset_tooltip: Reload weights from config file, discarding unsaved changes
comparison.recalculate_tooltip: Recalculate aggregate scores for this category using saved weights
comparison.modifiers_description: Score adjustments applied after weighted sum

modifier.bonus_hint: Flat score offset added after all weighted calculations. Use to compensate for categories that systematically score lower.
modifier.noise_tolerance_hint: Scales the noise penalty. 100% = full penalty (default), 0% = no penalty, 30% = lenient (recommended for wildlife/sports).
modifier.clipping_multiplier_hint: Multiplies the highlight clipping penalty. 100% = standard, 150% = stricter (default for 'default' category), 0% = disabled.
modifier.skip_clipping_penalty_hint: Disables the highlight clipping penalty entirely. Default: enabled for silhouette (backlit subjects).
modifier.skip_oversaturation_penalty_hint: Disables the oversaturation penalty. Default: enabled for night, astro, concert categories.
modifier.apply_blink_penalty_hint: Applies a score multiplier when a blink is detected. Default: enabled for portrait, portrait_bw, group_portrait.

filter.required_tags_hint: At least one tag must match (any mode) or all must match (all mode) for a photo to reach this category.
filter.excluded_tags_hint: Photos with any of these tags are excluded, even when required tags match.
filter.tag_match_mode_hint: How required tags are evaluated: 'Any' = one tag is enough, 'All' = every listed tag must be present.
filter.has_face_hint: Restrict to photos with (Yes) or without (No) at least one detected face.
filter.is_monochrome_hint: Restrict to B&W (Yes) or color-only (No) photos.
filter.is_silhouette_hint: Restrict to backlit subjects where face details are obscured.
filter.is_group_portrait_hint: Restrict to photos with 4 or more detected faces in frame.
filter.face_ratio_hint: Fraction of the frame covered by the largest face (0.0–1.0). e.g., min=0.05 → face ≥ 5% of frame.
filter.face_count_hint: Number of detected faces. e.g., min=2 for multi-person shots.
filter.iso_hint: Camera ISO sensitivity. e.g., max=800 to restrict to low-noise shots.
filter.shutter_speed_hint: Exposure time in seconds. e.g., max=0.02 for action/sports (≥ 1/50 s).
filter.focal_length_hint: Lens focal length in mm. e.g., min=100 for telephoto/wildlife shots.
filter.f_stop_hint: Aperture f-number. Lower = wider aperture. e.g., max=2.8 for shallow depth-of-field.
filter.luminance_hint: Mean image brightness (0.0–1.0). e.g., max=0.15 restricts to night/low-light scenes.

validation.noise_tolerance_range: Noise tolerance must be between 0% and 200%
validation.clipping_multiplier_range: Clipping multiplier must be between 0% and 500%
validation.shutter_range: Shutter speed must be between 0 and 60 seconds

suggestion.aesthetic: Consider stronger visual impact through better lighting or subject matter
suggestion.tech_sharpness: Use a faster shutter speed or tripod to improve sharpness
suggestion.face_quality: Ensure the face is well-lit and in focus
suggestion.eye_sharpness: Focus precisely on the eyes for portraits
suggestion.face_sharpness: Ensure the face region is sharp — avoid motion blur
suggestion.comp_score: Try applying compositional rules like rule of thirds or leading lines
suggestion.exposure_score: Adjust exposure to avoid clipping highlights or crushing shadows
suggestion.color_score: Consider white balance correction or more vibrant color grading
suggestion.contrast_score: Increase tonal contrast for more visual depth
suggestion.noise_sigma: Use a lower ISO or apply noise reduction
suggestion.dynamic_range_stops: Bracket exposures or use graduated filters for better dynamic range
suggestion.leading_lines_score: Look for natural lines that draw the eye into the frame
suggestion.subject_sharpness: Ensure your main subject is the sharpest element in the frame
suggestion.subject_prominence: Give the subject more frame space or use a shallower depth of field
suggestion.bg_separation: Use wider aperture or greater distance to separate subject from background
suggestion.liqe_score: Improve overall image quality — check for distortions or artifacts
suggestion.isolation_bonus: Use wider aperture to better isolate the subject from background

culling.title: Culling
culling.confirm_tooltip: Keep selected photos, discard the rest
culling.skip_tooltip: Move to next group without deciding
culling.auto_select_all_tooltip: Automatically keep the best photo in each remaining group
culling.burst_help: Photos taken in rapid succession are grouped together. Click photos to keep them (green border), unselected photos (red border) will be hidden. The best photo is auto-selected based on sharpness and quality.
culling.similar_help: Visually similar photos across your library are grouped using AI embeddings. Review each group, keep the best shots, and discard near-duplicates. Adjust the threshold slider to control how similar photos must be to form a group.
culling.help_text: Review groups of similar or burst photos. Click to select, double-click to select exclusively. Confirmed groups hide rejected photos from the gallery.
